# GitBook Pricing 2026 - Per-Site Model

## Summary

GitBook transitioned from user-based to per-site pricing. Current tiers: Free ($0, 1 user, includes block editor + GitHub/GitLab sync + API playgrounds + LLM optimizations + preview deployments), Premium ($65/month per site + $12/user for additional users, adds custom domain, AI search, analytics, user feedback), Ultimate ($249/month per site + $12/user, adds AI Assistant with 500 answers, AI Insights, GitBook Agent, authenticated access, site sections, adaptive content), Enterprise (custom, adds SAML SSO, dedicated support, custom integrations, unlimited adaptive content, white-glove migration).

## Key quotations / statistics

- Free: "Block-based visual editor, GitHub/GitLab sync, Interactive API playgrounds, Preview deployments, LLM optimizations"
- Premium: "$65/month per site + $12/user - Custom domain and branding, AI search, Advanced analytics and user feedback, Site redirects"
- Ultimate: "$249/month per site + $12/user - AI Assistant (500 included answers), AI Insights and GitBook Agent, Authenticated access, Adaptive content"
- Enterprise: "SAML SSO, Dedicated support and account manager, Custom integrations, White-glove migration service, Unlimited adaptive content"
- Recent change: "GitBook recently transitioned from a user-based to a site-based pricing model"
- AI features became increasingly prominent across tiers

## Annotations for stinger-forge

- GitBook's per-site pricing ($65/month Premium, $249/month Ultimate) positions it between Mintlify Pro ($300/month for the whole team) and Docusaurus ($0 self-hosted). For small teams needing one public docs site with decent AI search, Premium at $65/month is compelling.
- The Free tier is generous (GitHub sync, API playgrounds, preview deploys) - document as a strong starting point.
- GitBook's block-based editor (Notion-like) is a major differentiator for non-technical content contributors compared to MDX-only alternatives.
- Adaptive content (Ultimate/Enterprise) is a 2026 feature unique to GitBook in this space.
- Limitation vs Mintlify: GitBook's customization depth is lower; white-label requires Enterprise.
- Add to `guides/00-platform-selection.md` comparison table alongside Mintlify and Docusaurus.
